title: Separation of Powers
lead: Coined by ==Montesquieu== in ==L'Esprit des Lois (1748)==. ==Art 50== directs the State to separate the judiciary from the executive. India has a ==separation of functions more than powers==.

items: ==Preventing tyranny== — no single group accumulates arbitrary power
==Efficiency & specialisation== — each organ masters its core function

sections.title: Why It's Different in a Parliamentary Democracy

items.title: Fusion, Not Separation
bullets: CoM are ==members of Parliament== (Arts 74/75, 163/164), surviving on the House's confidence — unlike the wholly separate US President and Congress

items.title: Judiciary as the Rigid Check
bullets: With political organs overlapping, ==judicial review (Arts 13, 32, 226/227)== is the primary guarantor
[[Ram Jawaya v. Punjab (1955)]]: no ==absolute rigidity==, but functions are sufficiently differentiated

items.title: Accountability Replaces Separation
bullets: ==Question Hour==, no-confidence motions, committees, debates, ==budgetary control==, censure motions

items.title: Cooperation Is Built In
bullets: Government introduces bills → Parliament debates and passes → executive implements
India: ==separation with checks and balances==, boundaries drawn by the Constitution

head: Organ
Separation
Overlap / checks
rows: ==Executive==
Pres/Gov as heads (Arts 52/153); executive power (Arts 73/162); conduct of business (Arts 77/166); can't be legislators (Arts 59/158); SC consultation (Art 143); services (Arts 309–310)

==Legislature==: part of it (Arts 79/168); RS nominations; summon/prorogue/dissolve (Arts 85/174); addresses & messages (Arts 86–87/175–176); ==assent to bills (Arts 111, 200)==; budget (Arts 112/202); ==ordinances (Arts 123/213)==; Art 356–357. ==Judiciary==: pardons (Arts 72/161); immunity (Art 361); ==judicial appointments (Arts 124/217)==
==Legislature==
Composition (Arts 79/168); legislative powers (Arts 245–248); own procedure (Arts 118/208)

==Executive==: elects President (Art 54); impeachment (Art 61); CoM aid & advice (Arts 74/163). ==Judiciary==: courts can't probe proceedings (Arts 122/212) — but [[Kihoto Hollohan (1992)]], [[Nabam Rebia (2016)]]; privileges bounded by [[Sita Soren (2024)]]; water disputes exclude SC (Art 262); ==contempt power== is quasi-judicial
==Judiciary==
Own procedure (Arts 145/227); HC supervises lower courts; ==collegium== (2nd & 3rd Judges cases)

==Judicial review== (Arts 13, 32, 226); ordinances — [[DC Wadhwa (1987)]]; pardons — [[Shatrughan Chauhan (2014)]]; assent timelines — TN Governor case 2025; anti-defection — Kihoto; privileges — Sita Soren

head: Case
What it settled
rows: [[Ram Jawaya v. Punjab (1955)]]
No ==absolute rigidity==; organs sufficiently differentiated — one organ cannot assume another's essential functions.
[[Kesavananda Bharati (1973)]]
==Separation of powers is basic structure==; constituent power coextensive with, not superior to, the people's will.
[[Indira Gandhi v. Raj Narain (1975)]]
Struck the 39th CA — ==first application of basic structure==; free elections and judicial review upheld.
[[Minerva Mills (1980)]]
==Judicial review is basic structure== — check on self-aggrandising amendments.
[[Kihoto Hollohan (1992)]]
Speaker's anti-defection decisions ==subject to judicial review==.
[[Naz Foundation (2009)]] · [[Navtej Johar (2018)]]
==Constitutional morality== over popular morality — judiciary as ==counter-majoritarian check==.
[[Sabarimala (2018)]]
Constitutional morality overrides customary morality — and showed the ==limits of intervention== amid social resistance.
[[Puttaswamy (2017)]] · [[Joseph Shine (2018)]]
Privacy and autonomy grounded in ==dignity over legislative/social morality==.
[[NJAC Case (2015)]]
99th CA struck down for ==judicial independence== — criticised as the judiciary protecting its own turf.
[[SR Bommai (1994)]]
==Art 356 subject to judicial review== — restraining executive overreach into the federal structure.

blocks.text: Separation of powers is not a wall — it is a living system of constitutional restraint and dialogue. Without it, the Constitution is just ink; with it, it becomes justice.
